item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1213's image was removed because we could not properly determine whether or not the image was properly licensed or attributed. It may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact Apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, let me get this straight; a scientist opened an extra dimensional portal to a land of animate inanimate objects. He then accidentally let a "baby" catapult go through the portal, and couldn't get it back. It then adopts the catapult, names it Hurley, until an accident happens when Hurley flings…"Robert," not sure who that is, then the scientist runs away and leaves it orphaned?

Okay, I'm giving this a +1, a) because the scientist named it Hurley, and I find that so hilarious and cute, and b) because you somehow made me feel sorry for something whose entire existence consists of catapulting babies into walls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That "research log" almost felt like generic mad scientist CliffsNotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That final log is so chilling - even more so because of the expungement of most of the text - that I couldn't help but upvote:

found Su[…] splatt[…] Robert like a tomato […], oh my god.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have been informed that I am now SCP-1213-2. I'm not sure how I feel about this. XD

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one makes me sad :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh God this is horrifying and hilarious and sad. I love it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This takes me back to my childhood, watching The Brave Little Toaster and totally missing the point of the movie but loving it all the same. That alone deserves a +1, and it's a great teaser for future articles to boot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks guys, I am currently working on another SCP, involving the same Dr. as this one, with more inanimate objects, so hopefully that may sate you.

In the meantime, if you haven't seen it, there is this: http://www.scp-wiki.net/little-hurley-lost-in-meatspace which is a tale I wrote based on this SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know right!? I upvoted it before, and now I want to upvote it again, and it won't let me.
……
Oh my god I now want to find that dimension's version of the Sun Cannon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I went, "It's a kittypult", and then I wavered as to whether that was a good thing.

Then I found out that it is in fact a kittypult that launches dwarves and babies, and that comes from a dimension of animate objects.

I upvoted with the fury of a thousand suns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
AAAnd upvoted the poor babypult

:'(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the general idea but the back story didn't seem to fit. The idea of this guy "adopting" the catapult seemed ridiculous in a -j sort of way while the beginning seems like your standard SCP. I think if you changed one to better fit the other this would be perfect. Still pretty good though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…..HURLEY?!?!?!?!?

Screw you, and take my up-vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to thank Lycan for inspiration, encouragement, and the latest round of edits.

As well as for being awesome.

:D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know whether this is specifically pointed at my SCP, i'll assume it is, but i've fixed the expungement with the rogue Dr. thing.

As for the Dr.'s notes, I could easily have written a whole thing, but as Scantron said, I didn't want to use any more words than I needed to, and I also wanted to enforce the idea of the laboratory and the notes being almost destroyed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you're right, should be fond, changing now

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you guys are right, changing to transported

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Log has the line

I've grown found of it,

I'm assuming it should say

I've grown fond of it,

I haven't corrected it because it's in a non-foundation character's log so I'm not entirely sure whether you wanted this guy to misspell it or not.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's just too silly for me, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This thing is a self-propelled Orcapult running around flinging children and small creatures instead of gobbo.
I don't know if the Warhammer image in my head is making me like this more than I should, but I'm almost definitely liking this more than I should. Especially the fact that it PURRS upon successful splatification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
after a launching it will remain stationary, emitting a "contented purring sound" while it resets its firing arm,

And that's where I upvoted.

And yeah, seconding that teleportation by unknown means isn't really good tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The point (which is frequently lost in the rush to make a funny) is that any expungement which is vague enough to do this with is most likely also a bad expungement. And by "bad," I mean the result of the author hoping that the reader will do most of the work of being scary or interesting, instead of coming up with something scary or interesting to tell the reader. (Which was the case in the example you're responding to.) Alternately, it's bad because the author knows what's supposed to go there, but hasn't left enough clues in the text as to what that is, which is also a flaw and should be corrected.

There are also cases where people do this just to score a laugh at the author's expense, of course, but the other thing is more common.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To me it needs to be more creepy, it needs to feel more like a sinister, lurking predator. After all, the concept of being yanked onto this thing and being brutally smashed against a wall is pretty terrifying, it's just the thing has an almost comical edge to it.
Also I take issue with the use of the word "Teleport", especially if it's by "unknown means". Teleport carries connotations that, without proper evidence, a true scientist couldn't assert. A better word for me would be "transported" but that's my two cents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I get really fcking annoyed by people filling in the redactions,
Okay, I guess it's fine in the case of [REDACTED], because that's an individual word or verb/noun/adj phrase, and if you can make a funny with a limited number of words pasted into a sentence, good on you.
But in the case of [DATA EXPUNGED] you can theoretically fill in whole paragraphs, you can add subjunctive clauses or even make the preceding sentences the subjunctive. Hell, you could add entire excerpts of George Carlin material if you feel like it.
It's just a cheap shot, and not funny.
So blow me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This thoroughly entertains me, possibly because this is all I could think of the whole way through.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, the 'device' is destroyed so you'd have to track down Dr. ████████ first, and i have a few plans for him… EDIT: or her…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Jesus. That's a horrifying thought. And it's even in Britain already.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much improved with the edits. Now to upvote and ponder writing "Lord Blackwood in the Empire of the Catapults".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Holy shit it looks much better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll give that illegible thing ago and see how it looks, cheers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The research log might read more smoothly if you made a note at the beginning that illegible bits would be represented as something less obtrusive, like […]. E.g.:

I've grown found of it, it […] no family of my […] so long […] alone working […]

Maybe not. I don't actually like the research log in the first place, though I approve of trying to hint at the origin of the thing. I'm just trying to make it easier to parse.

"Contented purring sound" is troubling also, though I see why it's there too.

I dunno. This is pretty rough, but the idea fits my sensibilities well enough to upvote it (provisionally) anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
or any other mammal displaying neotenous traits,

Don't all humans display neotenous traits?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird yet scary because of the possibility of a world of these. Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Which bits are silly? i've fixed the whole rogue doctor thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is a bunch of really, really stupid expungement in here. If you changed or removed some of that, I would be delighted to upvote a cat-apult.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How about:

Its stuck!

you tough critic you

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
thanks, i've addressed all but the 2nd point, not sure how to fix that.

as for the fourth:

The device was the experimental work of Dr. ████████, who could not be located.

is what i've changed it to, short simple and sweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
[ILLEGIBLE]uck!

I don't know how fuck is illegible if you remove the f. Puck? Sluck? Luck? Pluck?

Other than that, don't know how to feel. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Smapti is 100% right here IMO.

But that could be because I didn't get anything at all good from the research log except for some giggle-worthy turns of phrase. I like the rest of it.

EDIT: Also, yeah, bad expungement.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "people with dwarfism"-should probably be subjects, thought it's not that bad. Overall, some of the sentences are a bit cumbersome, but the idea is cute and scenarios described amusing, so it's an upvote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe i'll mix them up, having prey in every sentence of a paragraph irks me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It has no distinguishing features or unusual physical properties.

I think we can infer this because you didn't mention any distinguishing properties.

and then SCP-1213 will resume its search for more prey.

A bit awkward sounding.

SCP-1213 has also been known to target people with dwarfism on occasion.

This is a nice touch.

The device had been worked on for several years by Dr. ████████, a scientist that had gone rogue after [DATA EXPUNGED]. Dr. ████████ could not be located.

This is some bad expungement.

Also, the words unknown and illegible are used too often in the description and addendum, respectively.

Overall, not bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Victim, maybe?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: thanks, changed target to prey, if anyone can think of a better word, please share.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The research log actually works for me; it gives the SCP a cool backstory that came out of nowhere without using any more words than it needs to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Goddammit, I actually like this. You need to change some punctuation around, like adding a semicolon in the middle of "searching for targets, should SCP-1213 find a suitable target" in the first description sentence. Also, you need a word other than "target" in all the places you use that; the target would be what the catapult is shooting at, not what it's shooting. Otherwise, I can actually get behind this, in an old-school SCP sort of way. The log even works for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, I have no idea how this is going to go, I had thought this was going to be a -J and it might still be, but i thought there was perhaps enough here for it to make it - we will see.

The picture there is the only good one i could find that fit what i needed, tell me if you know of a better one.

Thanks in advance for your critique.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The device had been worked on for several years by Dr. ████████, a scientist that had gone rogue after [DATA EXPUNGED].

"The device had been worked on for several years by Dr. Wily, a scientist that had gone rogue after stealing and reprogramming the Robot Masters."

I actually like the setup and the idea of this SCP, but it goes downhill when Dr. Redacto is introduced, and the research log is a steaming pile of [ILLEGIBLE].

I want to upvote this, but it's just not polished enough. No vote for now.
